channel.title: Comments for page "Personnel And Character Dossier"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Personnel And Character Dossier"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My suggestion for a dado entry in the "Persons of Interest" section:

dado: A para-"pharmaceutical" manufacturer. Sells items such as migraine medication, viagra, energy drinks, hair growth gel, donuts, and fast food.

Owns an interdimensional grocery store run by robots. Business partners with a hamster. May or may not have turned a man into a pepper.

Also known for having a broken upper case key.

Also, why is this page locked now?

EDIT: dado added, page has been unlocked

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chappell is one of my favorite characters on the site, both to read and to write about. Very happy that he finally has a blurb here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big Man Chappell now has the blurb he deserves!Yeah, the hide text is a Goodfellas quote, what are you gonna do about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disregard, issue resolved in IRC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my proposed revision:

+ show block
– hide block

Dr. Michael Edison: Level-3 Researcher, current head of the Foundation's Inter-Site Testing Initiative (ISTI). Dr. Edison has received disciplinary action on multiple occasions due to a repeated pattern of unsanctioned and ill-conceived behavior. These behaviors have resulted in temporary reassignment to the Site-██ Antarctic base. His current assignment at Site 19 is contingent on approval from his assigned therapist.

[[collapsible show="My legends begins in the 12th century…" hide="FOOL!"]]

Though quiet and reserved most of the time, Dr. Edison secretly dreams of being a badass like Dr. Clef (or at least the image most staffers have of Dr. Clef). While respected as a researcher, his attempts at more outrageous endeavors are most often met with derision. Once had a petty (but long-standing grudge) against a certain member of the maintenance staff, who later defected to the chaos insurgency for unrelated reasons. Is known to sometimes write terrible fanfiction.

I figure I might as well get rid of the part about the "metafiction department", since Pataphysics is kinda it's own thing and I haven't been involved with any of that writing. I instead, I shifted that role to being part of a cross-site testing initiative, since I've contributed to a lot to those logs and am currently part of the MAST team.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure, either post an edited version of the profile or PM a Rewrite Team staff member with the content to be changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… I just realized that Dr. Edison's personnel dossier has a reference to a certain character who's author left the Foundation under very inauspicious circumstances, and I think I'd rather not give that author more attention. Can I go back and edit the profile? I mean he is my character, after all.

item.title: Re: character
item.content:encoded: If a character has been used in three or more stories of any format the author or a person who has written about them may add the character at their leisure.

item.title: character
item.content:encoded: Am I allowed to place a character on this list/page?

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
still, we should have someone listed for that type of position in Site 17, it kind of seems weird that there isn't.

The Dossier exists to link to characters who have appeared in various works, not so much to provide a general overview of all the staff that should exist in the whole of the Foundation. If you feel a certain character really really needs to be on this list, you are encouraged to write material for them so they can be properly added.

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
SCP is about creating good stories, not trying to convince other writers to use your OCs when you "can't spend the time needed to create tales or SCPs with them."

Every collaborative element of SCP, from sites to characters to object classes, became a thing because someone used it in an interesting way that inspired others to use it as well. Posting about a life coach on page 5 of a forum thread isn't doing anything interesting with it yourself, nor is it anything that will inspire others to do anything interesting with it.

The same thing often happens with the MTF log page where people come up with ideas for teams they think are cool in concept but then lack either the will or the ability to actually use said teams in articles. It comes down to the same principle: character ideas in a vaccum lack worth. Anyone can have a concept, but you need to be willing and able to bring the concept to life in an entertaining way. And if you can't or won't, how is it fair to expect someone else to do it for you?

I actually think there's some interesting stories you could tell from the lens of a life coach, or featuring a life coach. So I encourage you to try writing those stories.

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
Then that was my mistake, thanks for fixing that obvious misunderstanding, still, we should have someone listed for that type of position in Site 17, it kind of seems weird that there isn't.

So he would be a; Dietary and Nutrition Specialist, that makes sense as there are humanoid SCPs in Site 17. Although I also feel that it would be better to tack on Mentoring Experience, as that would make him really useful in handling the ones that might not be fully mature yet.

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded: The discrepancy is the fact that the skillsets in the numbered list don't really map closely to the generic "life coach" job. They're way more duties than the standard counselor sort of position would be expected to fulfill.

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
That is why I said "Basically, someone to fulfill the duties of a Life Coach.", not him being an actual Life Coach.

Here is an excerpt from a draft I am making:

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-5827 is to be kept within a one room cell furnished with basic amenities. Constant surveillance is to be maintained, if there are any issues, contact Doctor Francis.

Addendum-5827-01: SCP-5827 is to be fed only raw chicken food from the diet plan that Doctor Francis has created and is to only drink water and milk.

item.title: Re: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
I would like to know if there is possibility for a Character Pickup section, as in, say if you have the concept for a character but can't spend the time needed to create tales or SCPs with them

I would say no. If you want your character on the mainsite, you should take the time and effort to write them up yourself, rather than just dumping the idea somewhere and expect someone else to do the legwork. We want to encourage authors to do their own writing. (Also, we don't want existing authors to get pestered with "can you write my character up please???!?!" requests.)

(ETA: …also, the premise you've got is… kind of nonsensical? Technically one doesn't really need much in the way of credentials to be a Life Coach. A reputable dietitian, on the other hand, would require a fair bit of training and depending on specialization, a certain degree.)

item.title: I have an idea of a character
item.content:encoded:
Alright, so I am in progress of creating a rewrite proposal for SCP-5827, in-case none of you know, there is a character in it that kind of shows that there is a lack of established personnel at Site 17, introducing Dr. Francis.

Ok, so what he showed we need:

  1. Dietary Specialist
  2. Activity Coordinator
  3. Nutritionary Specialist
  4. Well-Being Specialist

Basically, someone to fulfill the duties of a Life Coach.

Just putting this out there.

Also asking:

I would like to know if there is possibility for a Character Pickup section, as in, say if you have the concept for a character but can't spend the time needed to create tales or SCPs with them as a prominent force, if we had this section, with permission characters could be put in it for anyone to pick up and put into their works.

Kind of works like the whole thing being trying to divert attention towards idea threads. This could also work to help accelerate establishing a strong feel for the Personnel Dossier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intensive observation indicates Dr. King is irritated by his seemingly inherit effect.

I don't recall King's effect being genetic, so wouldn't this be `inherent effect` instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Personnel and Character Dossier and the O5 Command Dossier are, without a doubt, my favorite hubs of all the site ! Every time I read them, it's still a pleasure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a dead link in the second paragraph of Alexei Belitrov's profile. I don't know if it's a work in progress or if the linked tale got deleted; in the latter case, the link should be removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there a minimum upvote threshold that I must reach for character entries? If so, I can use some of my other works in which Dr. Martens is featured in.

Typically I'd encourage something that's at least in the positive double-digits, just to be on the completely safe side. So far as a universal minimum upvote threshold goes, I don't think there's one that everyone agrees on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the entry.

Also, the tale has already been posted since Jam Con 2019, and it’s stagnated at +6 since then.

Is there a minimum upvote threshold that I must reach for character entries? If so, I can use some of my other works in which Dr. Martens is featured in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that the last tale is only at +6, this entry feels a bit premature?

Also I think the alphabetical order is messed up with your entry?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added my own character, Dr. Reese Martens, mainly from this SCP, this tale, and this tale.

item.title: He Ain't No Two-Footed Northern Fifter!
item.content:encoded: "Big Cheese" Horace has been added!

item.title: Re: Can Dr. Nukea technically make it?
item.content:encoded:
I would say no, your character does not count.

The collaboration logs don't get tagged with researcher names since they aren't the focus of the piece. Similarly, if a character's name is just mentioned in a tale but the tale overall doesn't feature the character prominently, the tag would not be applied.

You would need to write multiple full-length pieces that involve your character for them to qualify for a listing here.

item.title: Can Dr. Nukea technically make it?
item.content:encoded:
So I read the guideline of three stories and

one

two

three

I put these up because I’m not sure if he counts or not.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Someone added the page when they shouldn't have, since the character does not qualify for a character tag and the one page they were referenced in has long since been deleted. I have reverted the edits for that entry. Thank you for bringing that to staff attention!

In the future, for a quicker response on unauthorized edits, feel free to send me a wikidot PM or go to the IRC chatroom to notify a staff member.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the janitor Byrd entry still a thing? Was it added as a joke or just someone edited the page and nobody has noticed that it was added.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To date, the Foundation has been unable to confirm any information regarding The Man With The Upside-Down Face, including whether or not his face is actually upside-down.

This got a chuckle from me. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Realized I've been neglecting this for too long.

Finally made an entry for Icky & Manny, and added a link to 'So They Called Him Darke' in the MC&D section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a lad, ol' Chaz Ambrose from one, two and three stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that- that was dumb of me. It's Dr. Samuel M. Lloyd, from this tale, this SCP, and this SCP. Apologies >_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "a dude" for POI of the year 2018.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
desperately hoping I haven't broken any rules

I mean, it'd be easier to confirm if you gave us more of an identifier than "a dude", and some links to pieces they featured in :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a dude / desperately hoping I haven't broken any rules

item.title: Re: Is kondraki's disappearance officially canon?
item.content:encoded:
Preface: I am the original writer of a good chunk of this dossier, including the Kondraki section, and I am the editor of Resurrection.

If we base reality with scp files (aka the real series I through V) then what is stated in those files should be considered true, right?

No!

Some are from alternate timelines.

Some are disinformation (but we'll never know which).

Some are simply incorrect.

Is kondraki's disappearance officially canon?

No!

This dossier presents what most people in the active writer community who write those characters (and, failing that, the authors or 'major' writers of a character) think is true about a character. It's the "accepted narrative" and nothing else. It doesn't have to be true at all. In-universe, it could be anything from disinformation to a joke at Kondraki's expense, or just not canon at all to tales & SCPs that write Kondraki as an active character.

Kondraki's disappearance is there because Real Life Kondraki quit the community under less-than-ideal circumstances, and most people quit writing the character on the main sites, for various reasons. Though he definitely still pops up here and there! The 'retired' section is effectively meta-commentary, nothing more.

"The King Is Dead" is canon to Resurrection specifically because, even though it wasn't meant to be canon to the Original Site Meta-Narrative when it was written, it became canon to that meta-narrative by accident (by default). But the Original Site Meta-Narrative hasn't been the final word on the "true story" of the Foundation since 2010. Eight whole years. And even before that, it was shaky.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Alainpbat, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

If we base reality with scp files (aka the real series I through V) then what is stated in those files should be considered true, right?

Some SCP articles contradict others.

item.title: Re: Is kondraki's disappearance officially canon?
item.content:encoded:
And yes, there is no canon, but we kinda have to acknowledge reality when constructing a universe right? Because if 231 is an scp, then clef-kondraki is probable. If we base reality with scp files (aka the real series I through V) then what is stated in those files should be considered true, right?
Which brings me back to kondraki's disappearance, cause, to the best of my knowledge, him gone, is a manifestation after we wrote "the king is dead" which was later used in resurection…

item.title: Is kondraki's disappearance officially canon?
item.content:encoded:
Because it horrifies me that we're letting this character go (albeit temporarily)
Also, are these members still writers in real life or have they gone away eg: gears

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solution: go the route I did with Alexei and acknowledge that there's multiple canons involving the character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I would rather go with SCP-3001's Scranton.

This seems to be unfair to the other authors who write different Scrantons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I would rather go with SCP-3001's Scranton. Because it said (even though there is no canon) he invented the SRA's and maybe be SCP-106, which might make it more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trouble is, there's multiple characters named that all of whom have completely incompatible canons. CWW's junior researcher Scranton, FFB's Scranton who lived and made reality anchors in the 19th century, and Goomy's Scranton who got trapped in 3001. With the details given, none of these could be the same character, but at the same time each successive iteration is an obvious reference to the previous. It's kind of a nightmare for a page like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone should add Dr. Robert Scranton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roger, so its write SCP, then reference Character here?

Yes. Or tale. Basically, the character needs to already feature noticeably on the site before they get an entry on this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the character's backstory is instrumental to the SCP, see how you can work it in to that. If it's not, save it for later. That said, I would highly suggest you seek feedback from the Ideas and Brainstorming forum before going much further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roger, so its write SCP, then reference Character here? I was hoping to have a character with some backstory before hand, just for my SCP to work logically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This page is only for characters that have already been written or referenced in other works on the site. It is not for creating new characters. In the future, please contact staff if you want to add someone to this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I created a new Foundation Agent to use in a future SCP. "Agent Grey"

Please check it out and let me know if any changes need to be made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: whys that scranton guy not on here?? ive seen his name around a lot and though i dont know who he actually is he seems like an important guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverted because a character was added that (as far as I can tell) had no stories attached to them. Noting this for the record.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added Kenneth Spencer and Robin Thorne from my Unusual Investigations series to the Persons of Interest tab. Since they've only appeared as a duo so far, I just did a single entry for both of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added this excerpt on the portion on Alexei Belitrov

Additionally, in Reality Construct FZ27GO, multiple persons resembling Alexei Belitrov spontaneously manifested, and were frequently sighted fighting at nighttime. In most occasions, these iterations of Alexei Belitrov are accompanied by a unidentified human with a red tattoo on one of their hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for letting staff know! I've changed the link to using three brackets and the path name, rather than the full url. Should work now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've found a malformed link in Dr Katherine Sinclair's entry. The Site-87 link is missing a colon.
It is currently: http//www.scp-wiki.net/the-s-c-plastics-hub
It should be: http://www.scp-wiki.net/the-s-c-plastics-hub

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hope you guys don't mind; I added a little thing for Dr. Charles Anborough.

SCP-1649 - Me
SCP-2400 - Me
Footsteps - Me
Exit History - FortuneFavorsBold
Too Insane to Fail - TobiasTheTapir

item.title: Re: Kind Man Backstory
item.content:encoded: I think, if I remember their draft board correctly, they're writing The Kind Man into an as a SCP article.
